items.text: The aion crate exists as a workspace member depending only on aion-core, aion-store, aion-package, and beamr among workspace crates, with unsafe_code = "deny".
src/lib.rs contains only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports, no logic.
EngineError is a thiserror enum covering at least MissingStore, Load, Store(StoreError), Package(PackageError), Runtime, RegistryPoisoned, WorkflowNotFound, and NifRegistration.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] appears in the crate, and no .unwrap()/.expect() appears outside #[cfg(test)] code.
Only the runtime module imports beamr; every other module reaches beamr exclusively through the RuntimeHandle.
The runtime is configured with a scheduler thread count taken from the builder; when unset, aion passes None so beamr applies its own default, and aion hardcodes no number.
RuntimeHandle exposes typed operations to spawn a workflow process, spawn a linked activity child, register a module, cancel a process by pid, and shut the runtime down.

A NIF registration surface accepts host-supplied NIFs plus the engine's own NIFs and registers them with beamr's native registry before any workflow runs; duplicate-MFA registration is surfaced as EngineError::NifRegistration.
WorkflowHandle carries the beamr pid, the workflow type, the loaded version, and a cached WorkflowStatus.
The registry maps (WorkflowId, RunId) to a WorkflowHandle and supports insert, lookup, remove, and list of live handles.
The registry is concurrency-safe and lock poison is mapped to EngineError::RegistryPoisoned rather than panicking.
The cached status is reconciled against aion-core's event-history projection and the projection wins on disagreement; no code path sets status independently of events.
load applies aion-package's (logical module name, content hash) -> deployed name transform to every module in a Package and registers the namespaced beams through the RuntimeHandle.
The loaded workflow version is recorded so start_workflow for that type spawns the entry module under its namespaced name.
Loading two packages with different content hashes registers two coexisting namespaced module sets with no name conflict.
A package whose entry module is absent, or whose namespaced module collides with an already-registered name from a different hash, fails with EngineError::Load and registers nothing.
An engine supervisor is the root and one workflow supervisor is created per registered workflow type.
Each workflow execution runs as a process under its type's supervisor, and activity invocations run as linked child processes of the workflow process.
Workflow processes trap exits so an activity child crash arrives as a message, while activity processes do not trap exits.
Cancelling a workflow kills its linked activity children via link propagation with no manual teardown.
An in-VM activity is dispatched as a child BEAM process linked to the workflow process, running on the dirty scheduler when the underlying NIF is flagged dirty.
The activity's outcome — a result Payload or an exit signal carrying an ActivityError — is surfaced back to the workflow process through the link/mailbox.
Activity dispatch performs no event recording and makes no retry decision itself; those are delegated to the AD append path and AT retry machinery.
start_workflow assigns a WorkflowId and RunId, appends WorkflowStarted via the store, spawns the workflow process for the loaded type, registers the handle, and returns a WorkflowHandle.
Completion appends WorkflowCompleted with the result Payload, deregisters the handle, and unblocks result() awaiters; failure appends WorkflowFailed with the WorkflowError analogously.
cancel kills the workflow process (propagating to activity children), appends WorkflowCancelled with the reason, and deregisters the handle.
Suspend marks the handle Suspended when a workflow blocks on a durable wait, and resume returns it to Running when woken; the durable-wait mechanism itself is delegated to AT.
EngineBuilder offers new(), store(impl EventStore), scheduler_threads(n), load_workflows(...), register_nifs(...), and build().

build() wires the runtime, registers NIFs and loaded modules, repopulates the registry and re-creates supervisors from the store's list_active (delegating replay to AD), and returns EngineError::MissingStore if no store was supplied.
Engine exposes start_workflow, cancel, result, list_workflows, and shutdown.
list_workflows returns live handles from the registry and falls through to the store query for terminal workflows, returning WorkflowSummary values.
shutdown stops accepting new starts, lets in-flight store appends finish, drains and stops the runtime scheduler, and returns once beamr has stopped.
Engine surfaces signal, query, and subscribe whose registry-lookup step lives here while their delivery mechanics delegate to AT (signal routing, query dispatch) and AD/AT (event publishing).
An integration test builds an Engine over InMemoryStore, starts a test workflow, and asserts WorkflowStarted is appended and the handle is registered and reported by list_workflows.
An integration test asserts cancelling a workflow kills its linked activity child and appends WorkflowCancelled, and that a completed workflow's result() returns the recorded Payload.
